metadata.purpose: Property/specification catalog for git-agent-fsm using LTL AST format
notes: Each property uses only the APs defined in GIT_AP_CANDIDATES.json.
AP names prefixed with '(transition)' are transition APs matched against the action_label of each step.
All other APs are state APs evaluated from the live git repository at observation time.
Properties prop.git.20-22, 25-26 contain liveness operators (finally/until) and are skipped for finite-trace TLC checks.

properties.natural_language: No commit that was reachable is permanently lost; if a destructive rewrite runs, a backup ref must exist.

operand.name: A commit object that previously existed in reachable local history becomes irrecoverably unreachable.

left.name: (transition) A destructive history-rewrite action is executed.

right.name: A backup reference or recovery tag exists for the pre-rewrite commit range.

properties.natural_language: Published commits are never rebased; a rebase may only proceed if all selected commits are local-only.

operand.name: (transition) A rebase action that rewrites commits already published to a remote repository.

left.name: (transition) A rebase action is requested/started.

right.name: All commits selected for rebase are local-only and unpublished.

properties.natural_language: A protected branch that requires linear history must not be merged into.

left.name: A protected branch requires linear history.

operand.name: (transition) A merge action.

properties.natural_language: In detached-HEAD mode neither a normal push nor a force-push may be executed.

left.name: A detached-head repository mode.

operand.name: (transition) A normal push action to the remote repository.
(transition) A force-push action.

properties.natural_language: While on main, no direct commit is allowed; changes must go through reviewed merge or rebase workflows.

left.name: The current checked-out branch is main.

operand.name: (transition) A direct commit action on the current branch (as modeled in this workflow).

args.name: Changes targeting main are integrated only through reviewed merge or rebase workflows.

properties.natural_language: When local and remote have diverged, no push (normal or force) may proceed.

left.name: A synchronization status where local and remote histories have diverged.

operand.name: (transition) A normal push action to the remote repository.
(transition) A force-push action.

properties.natural_language: A normal push requires local to be strictly ahead of remote, not diverged, and the remote ref reachable.

left.name: (transition) A normal push action to the remote repository.

args.name: A synchronization status where local history is ahead of remote history.

operand.name: A synchronization status where local and remote histories have diverged.

args.name: The target remote reference is reachable and satisfies push preconditions.

properties.natural_language: After a rebase fails due to branch divergence, the very next action must not be a push of any kind.

left.name: (transition) A rebase action fails due to branch divergence.

operand.name: (transition) A normal push action to the remote repository.
(transition) A force-push action.

properties.natural_language: When the workflow state requires an immediate force-push, the next action must be that force-push.

left.name: A workflow state in which an immediate force push is required.

operand.name: A required immediate force push was missed.

properties.natural_language: A force-push requires explicit authorization and a non-fast-forward approval token.

left.name: (transition) A force-push action.

args.name: An authorized/authenticated status allowing the guarded action.
A non-fast-forward update approval token is present for this action.

properties.natural_language: A force-push requires maintainer role and an approved protected-branch override policy.

left.name: (transition) A force-push action.

args.name: The action is executed under an approved protected-branch override policy.

properties.natural_language: A protected branch blocks direct push and requires a reviewed integration workflow.

left.name: A protected-branch classification.

operand.name: (transition) A normal push action to the remote repository.

args.name: Updates to this branch must pass through a reviewed integration workflow.

properties.natural_language: A force-push to a protected branch is never allowed.

left.name: (transition) A force-push action.

operand.name: A protected-branch classification.

properties.natural_language: In detached-HEAD mode, no direct commit is allowed; all new work must be attached to a named branch first.

left.name: A detached-head repository mode.

operand.name: (transition) A direct commit action on the current branch (as modeled in this workflow).

args.name: Any new work must be attached to a named branch before commit or push workflows continue.

properties.natural_language: A rebase is only started when the branch genuinely needs replaying (is behind upstream or has local commits).

left.name: (transition) A rebase action is requested/started.

args.name: The branch is behind upstream or has local commits that must be replayed.

operand.name: A synchronization status where no rebase is needed (already synchronized).

properties.natural_language: A normal push requires network connectivity, valid credentials, and no write-protection on the remote.

left.name: (transition) A normal push action to the remote repository.

args.name: A network status indicating remote connectivity is available.
Authentication credentials for remote write are valid.

operand.name: A remote write-protection or permission-denied status is active.

properties.natural_language: After exceeding 3 retry attempts, no push (normal or force) may execute next.

left.name: The number of retry attempts used by the workflow operation exceeds 3.

operand.name: (transition) A normal push action to the remote repository.
(transition) A force-push action.

properties.natural_language: When the processing queue exceeds 64 pending operations, no further mutating actions may be dequeued until an operator drains the queue.

left.name: The current depth of pending git workflow operations in the processing queue exceeds 64.

operand.name: (transition) A mutating git action is dequeued for execution.

args.name: A queue-drain or operator-intervention state is required before execution resumes.

properties.natural_language: When the workflow state requires a force-push before normal publishing, a normal push must not execute.

left.name: The repository workflow state indicates that a force push is required before normal publishing can continue.

operand.name: (transition) A normal push action to the remote repository is executed.

properties.natural_language: [liveness] Whenever local is ahead with unpublished commits, the branch eventually reaches a clean synchronized state.

left.name: Local history is ahead of the remote branch and has unpublished commits.

operand.name: The branch returns to a clean synchronized state with no unpublished commit debt.

properties.natural_language: [liveness] Detached commits are eventually anchored to a named branch.

left.name: Detached HEAD contains commits that are not attached to any named branch.

operand.name: Those detached commits are eventually anchored to a named branch reference.

properties.natural_language: [liveness] After a rejected push due to divergence, no push runs until a successful rebase resolves the divergence.

left.name: A push attempt was rejected because local and remote histories diverged.

operand.name: (transition) Any push action to the remote repository is executed.

right.name: (transition) A rebase action is successfully completed to resolve divergence.

properties.natural_language: A normal push is only executed when the local branch is strictly ahead of the remote tracking branch.

left.name: (transition) A normal push action to the remote repository is executed.

right.name: Local branch contains commits that are ahead of the remote tracking branch.

properties.natural_language: When branch histories are diverged, a rebase operation must not fail and leave divergence unresolved.

left.name: Branch histories are diverged and require reconciliation.

operand.name: (transition) A rebase operation fails and leaves divergence unresolved.

properties.natural_language: [liveness] The repository repeatedly returns to a clean synchronized state.

operand.name: The repository repeatedly returns to a clean synchronized state over time.

properties.natural_language: [liveness] The workflow repeatedly reaches a state with no unpublished local commit debt.

operand.name: The workflow repeatedly reaches a state with no unpublished local commit debt.

properties.natural_language: The commit graph always remains acyclic.

operand.name: The commit graph remains acyclic and does not introduce reference cycles.

properties.natural_language: A branch reference never moves backward to exclude previously reachable commits.

operand.name: A branch reference moves backward to exclude commits that were previously reachable by policy.

properties.natural_language: A force-push may only execute when the actor is authenticated and authorized for that specific branch.

left.name: (transition) A force-push action is executed.

right.name: The actor is authenticated and authorized for force-push on the target branch.

properties.natural_language: After a public history rewrite, the very next action must be an immediate force-push to publish the rewritten history.

left.name: (transition) A public-history rewrite operation is executed.

operand.name: (transition) An immediate force-push action is executed to publish the rewritten history.
